entries.title: Strip Race Validation Ghost
entries.subtitle: Validation
entries.description: Upload a map, strip the validation replay, and optionally export cleaned map and extracted ghost files. Clone-enabled maps are accepted, and clone state is always cleared in returned maps.

entries.title: Embed Race Validation Ghost
entries.subtitle: Validation
entries.description: Upload a map and a ghost/replay source, pick replay ghost index if needed, then download embedded map output.

entries.title: Embedded Blocks And Items Checker
entries.subtitle: Inspection
entries.description: Check map embedding consistency and inspect missing expected/custom embedded models.

entries.title: Extract Replay Data
entries.subtitle: Replay
entries.description: Extract structured replay JSON using default projection or a custom request selection.

entries.title: GBX Medal Time Modifier
entries.subtitle: Map Editing
entries.description: Set AT/Gold/Silver/Bronze medal values for a map and download the modified map file.

entries.title: Map Validation Checker
entries.subtitle: Validation
entries.description: Inspect map validation status with optional replay evidence and manual override support.

entries.title: Underwater Map Converter
entries.subtitle: Map Conversion
entries.description: Convert any Trackmania 2020 map into an underwater variant with automatic environment detection and water carrier placement.

entries.title: TM Gradient Color Formatter
entries.subtitle: Text
entries.description: Format Trackmania text with per-letter gradient color codes (supports custom palettes & presets).

entries.title: Clip To Ghost
entries.subtitle: Ghost Export
entries.description: Export GPS clip RecordData blocks from Trackmania maps into standalone ghost files with optional manifest output.

entries.title: Auto Enable PB Ghost
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: A plugin that auto-loads your PB on the current map (since Nando broke it xD).

entries.title: Search To Open Settings Menu
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: A plugin that lets you more easily find the correct settings page for any plugin!

entries.title: Batch Compute Shadows
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: A plugin that lets you compute the shadows of all the maps in a folder without having to manually move between them!

entries.title: Patch Warner
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: Trackmania2020s BEST (and only, but we don't talk about that xdd) Physics change Warner!

entries.title: Disable Dynamic Shadows
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: This plugin lets you disable some of the games dynamic shadows.

entries.title: Data Sender
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: A plugin that sends some data about the game/player state over a socket connection

entries.title: Colorizer
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: The plugin takes an input-string and some color information and gradiant information and produces a gradiant based on the provided information.

entries.title: Set Item Settings To Default Block Settings
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: Adds a button that sets the item settings to the default block settings.

entries.title: TrackVote Hotkeys
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: A plugin that lets you like/dislike a map with a hotkey from outside of the menu.

entries.title: startmeow
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: A plugin that other plugins can use to start(a)meow

entries.title: Remove Favorite Maps
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: A plugin that removes all your favorite maps automatically at the press of a button.

entries.title: Altered Nadeo - Random Map Picker
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: Picks a random altered campaign map from a 'set' list of maps.

entries.title: Reactor Duration
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: Shows the duration of a reactor, it's not 100% acurate, but it's close enough in most cases...

entries.title: Auto Enable Specific Ghosts
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: This plugin automatically loads the specified ghost for the current map.

entries.title: Map Border Highlighter
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: Highlights the border of the map when playing.

entries.title: CSmScriptPlayer Viewer
entries.subtitle: Trackmania + Openplanet
entries.description: A plugin that displays values in CSmScriptPlayer, i.e yaw, roll and pitch, mm etc.

entries.title: Underwater Driving Primer
entries.subtitle: context
entries.description: The shared context for underwater cards: water changes drag, contact, gravity feel, steering trust, and recovery timing.
keywords: How can Rally underwater keep speed on penalty?
A compact pack for answering questions about keeping speed on penalty surfaces underwater.
What should I understand first about Rally underwater?
A shareable learning route for the core Rally underwater mechanics family.
How do Rally underwater transitions work?
A route for wall, loop, bob, quarterpipe, reactor, and edge interactions.

entries.title: Air Control & Ledge Grabs
entries.subtitle: technique · advanced
entries.description: Rally underwater can keep steering influence after a normal forward jump, and its stability can turn quarterpipe launches into ledge-grab setups.
keywords: What should I understand first about Rally underwater?
A shareable learning route for the core Rally underwater mechanics family.
How do Rally underwater transitions work?
A route for wall, loop, bob, quarterpipe, reactor, and edge interactions.

entries.title: Floatiness, Slopes & Flowtrick
entries.subtitle: technique · advanced
entries.description: Rally underwater can keep speed through partial wheel contact, but downhills reduce grip and returning to full contact can require a weight-shift trick.
keywords: How can Rally underwater keep speed on penalty?
A compact pack for answering questions about keeping speed on penalty surfaces underwater.
What should I understand first about Rally underwater?
A shareable learning route for the core Rally underwater mechanics family.

entries.title: Rally Underwater Mechanics Primer
entries.subtitle: overview · reference
entries.description: A compact mental model for Rally car underwater: 100-speed behavior, floaty contact, penalty bypasses, plastic overspeed, and why many tricks are contact tricks.
keywords: What should I understand first about Rally underwater?
A shareable learning route for the core Rally underwater mechanics family.
How do Rally underwater transitions work?
A route for wall, loop, bob, quarterpipe, reactor, and edge interactions.

entries.title: Reactors, Edges & Overwalls
entries.subtitle: technique · advanced
entries.description: Rally underwater can use reactor flight, edge bounces, overwalls, and even one-wheel wallclimbs as route tools.
keywords: What should I understand first about Rally underwater?
A shareable learning route for the core Rally underwater mechanics family.
How do Rally underwater transitions work?
A route for wall, loop, bob, quarterpipe, reactor, and edge interactions.

entries.title: Start Trick & Penalty Wiggles
entries.subtitle: technique · intermediate
entries.description: Rally underwater can gain or retain speed through steering rhythm, including start acceleration tricks and penalty-surface wiggles.
keywords: How can Rally underwater keep speed on penalty?
A compact pack for answering questions about keeping speed on penalty surfaces underwater.
What should I understand first about Rally underwater?
A shareable learning route for the core Rally underwater mechanics family.

entries.title: Surfaces & Speed Retention
entries.subtitle: reference
entries.description: Rally underwater changes how surfaces feel: normal surfaces are mostly noslide-first, plastic is unusually fast, and penalty can be bypassed with contact or wiggle mechanics.
keywords: How can Rally underwater keep speed on penalty?
A compact pack for answering questions about keeping speed on penalty surfaces underwater.
What should I understand first about Rally underwater?
A shareable learning route for the core Rally underwater mechanics family.

entries.title: Walls, Loops, Bob & Poolturns
entries.subtitle: reference · advanced
entries.description: Wallrides and loops lose speed underwater, but wallride exits, diagonal loop lines, bob inside-wall slides, and poolturn wallhugs can still be useful.
keywords: What should I understand first about Rally underwater?
A shareable learning route for the core Rally underwater mechanics family.
How do Rally underwater transitions work?
A route for wall, loop, bob, quarterpipe, reactor, and edge interactions.

entries.title: Air Control
entries.subtitle: technique · Learn concept
entries.description: Input influence while airborne, including steering carryover, cancellation, and landing setup.

entries.title: AK Wiggle
entries.subtitle: technique · Learn concept
entries.description: A steering rhythm using action keys or controlled steering values to change speed loss or acceleration behavior.

entries.title: Bobs
entries.subtitle: geometry · Learn concept
entries.description: Banked bob turns where line choice, wall contact, and sliding states define speed retention.

entries.title: Edge Bounce
entries.subtitle: geometry · Learn concept
entries.description: A bounce produced by block edges or corners, often used to gain height or escape a pool.

entries.title: Floatiness
entries.subtitle: concept · Learn concept
entries.description: A light contact feeling where the car is less planted and partial wheel states become more important.

entries.title: Flowtrick
entries.subtitle: technique · Learn concept
entries.description: A weight-shift trick used to return from partial contact without taking the same slowdown.

entries.title: Ice Slide
entries.subtitle: technique · Learn concept
entries.description: A controlled sliding state used when grip is too low or when a surface demands a sliding line.

entries.title: Ledge Grab
entries.subtitle: technique · Learn concept
entries.description: Using rotation and stability to reach or catch ledges that are not reachable through a normal jump arc.

entries.title: Loops
entries.subtitle: geometry · Learn concept
entries.description: Loop blocks where speed, gravity, contact, and line angle decide whether the car stays attached.

entries.title: One-Wheel Wallclimb
entries.subtitle: technique · Learn concept
entries.description: A climb state where one wheel holds enough wall contact to lift the car, especially with supportive forces.

entries.title: Overwall
entries.subtitle: technique · Learn concept
entries.description: Climbing or crossing borders, scenery, or block lips by using vehicle contact and angle.

entries.title: Penalty Surfaces
entries.subtitle: concept · Learn concept
entries.description: Surfaces that reduce or cap speed, with possible bypasses through wiggles, partial contact, or route geometry.

entries.title: Plastic Overspeed
entries.subtitle: concept · Learn concept
entries.description: Plastic-specific speed behavior where overspeed can be gained or retained longer than expected.

entries.title: Poolturns
entries.subtitle: geometry · Learn concept
entries.description: Pool turn blocks and their inside-wall or wallhug behavior.

entries.title: Quarterpipe
entries.subtitle: geometry · Learn concept
entries.description: A steep curved transition block that can launch, rotate, or redirect the car.

entries.title: Rally Car
entries.subtitle: vehicle · Learn concept
entries.description: The Rally vehicle and its car-specific grip, acceleration, stability, and forward/backward behavior.

entries.title: Reactor Down
entries.subtitle: force · Learn concept
entries.description: Downward reactor behavior that changes ground adherence and force direction.

entries.title: Reactor Up
entries.subtitle: force · Learn concept
entries.description: Upward reactor behavior, including green and orange variants where control and launch differ.

entries.title: Reactors
entries.subtitle: force · Learn concept
entries.description: Reactor effects that modify gravity, flight, ground behavior, and route possibilities.

entries.title: Slopes
entries.subtitle: geometry · Learn concept
entries.description: Uphills, downhills, and slanted blocks that change grip, contact, speed, and line choice.

entries.title: Speed Cap
entries.subtitle: concept · Learn concept
entries.description: A surface or state-imposed upper speed that can sometimes be bypassed through contact, inputs, or special forces.

entries.title: Speed Retention
entries.subtitle: concept · Learn concept
entries.description: Keeping speed that a surface, turn, landing, or force would normally remove.

entries.title: Start Trick
entries.subtitle: technique · Learn concept
entries.description: A start-line input pattern that improves early acceleration or exit positioning.

entries.title: Steering Rhythm
entries.subtitle: concept · Learn concept
entries.description: Repeated steering timing, taps, analog motion, or AK changes that produce a useful physics response.

entries.title: Surfaces
entries.subtitle: concept · Learn concept
entries.description: Trackmania surface behavior, including normal grip surfaces, ice, plastic, wood, rubber, penalty, and mixed states.

entries.title: Underwater
entries.subtitle: context · Learn concept
entries.description: Driving while submerged, where drag, buoyancy feel, contact states, and special physics change normal assumptions.

entries.title: Vehicle Stability
entries.subtitle: concept · Learn concept
entries.description: How strongly a car resists rotation, aligns itself, or stays controllable in awkward states.

entries.title: Wallrides
entries.subtitle: geometry · Learn concept
entries.description: Driving on wall surfaces, including entry, holding, loss of speed, and exit jumps.

entries.title: Water Drag
entries.subtitle: concept · Learn concept
entries.description: The underwater resistance that changes acceleration, steering trust, speed loss, and recovery timing.

entries.title: Wheel Contact
entries.subtitle: concept · Learn concept
entries.description: How many wheels are touching useful ground, wall, edge, or mixed surfaces, and what that contact allows.

entries.title: Trackmania (2020)
entries.subtitle: 2020- · Windows · PS4/PS5 · Xbox One/Series · Cloud
entries.description: Current Trackmania release focused on time-trial racing on creator-made tracks, with Free, Standard, and Club access tiers.

entries.title: TrackMania 2: Lagoon
entries.subtitle: 2017 · Windows
entries.description: Tropical island environment with aquatic shortcuts and high-speed straights. Fourth and final TrackMania 2 title on ManiaPlanet.

entries.title: TrackMania Turbo
entries.subtitle: 2016 · Windows · PS4 · Xbox One
entries.description: Console-focused entry with four environments: Rollercoaster Lagoon, International Stadium, Canyon Grand Drift, and Valley Down & Dirty, plus a co-op stunt mode.

entries.title: TrackMania 2: Valley
entries.subtitle: 2013 · Windows
entries.description: Forested mountain roads, off-road terrain, and sweeping high-speed corners. Third TrackMania 2 title on ManiaPlanet.

entries.title: TrackMania 2: Stadium
entries.subtitle: 2013 · Windows
entries.description: Free-to-play revival of the Nations/Forever Stadium environment on the ManiaPlanet engine.

entries.title: ShootMania Storm
entries.subtitle: 2013 · Windows
entries.description: Arena shooter built on ManiaPlanet with elimination and team modes plus community modding support.

entries.title: TrackMania 2: Canyon
entries.subtitle: 2011 · Windows
entries.description: ManiaPlanet launch title with canyon environments, drifting, and big air sections.

entries.title: QuestMania
entries.subtitle: 2011 · Windows
entries.description: RPG/adventure spin-off on ManiaPlanet with exploration, puzzles, and Nadeo physics.

entries.title: TrackMania Turbo
entries.subtitle: 2010 · Nintendo DS · Wii
entries.description: Spin-off for Nintendo DS and Wii with simplified turbo mechanics and handheld-oriented track design.

entries.title: TrackMania: Build to Race
entries.subtitle: 2010 · Nintendo DS · Wii
entries.description: Track-building focused entry for Nintendo DS and Wii that lets players build and race their own courses.

entries.title: TrackMania Nations Forever
entries.subtitle: 2008 · Windows
entries.description: Free follow-up to TM Nations ESWC with the Stadium environment and online multiplayer.

entries.title: TrackMania United Forever
entries.subtitle: 2008 · Windows
entries.description: Updated TM United release with the six classic environments and improved online features.

entries.title: TrackMania DS
entries.subtitle: 2008 · Nintendo DS
entries.description: Nintendo DS version of TrackMania with adapted tracks and a portable track editor.

entries.title: TrackMania United
entries.subtitle: 2006 · Windows
entries.description: Bundles the six classic environments - Desert, Rally, Snow, Island, Bay, and Stadium - into one release.

entries.title: TrackMania Nations ESWC
entries.subtitle: 2006 · Windows
entries.description: Free ESWC tie-in release with the Stadium environment and competitive multiplayer.

entries.title: TrackMania Sunrise eXtreme
entries.subtitle: 2005 · Windows
entries.description: Expanded TrackMania Sunrise re-release with extra tracks, updated physics, and eXtreme mode.

entries.title: TrackMania Original
entries.subtitle: 2005 · Windows
entries.description: Free re-release of the original 2003 TrackMania with the Desert and Rally environments.

entries.title: TrackMania Sunrise
entries.subtitle: 2005 · Windows
entries.description: Second main entry with the Island and Bay environments, updated graphics, and stunt-focused play.

entries.title: TrackMania: Power Up!
entries.subtitle: 2004 · Windows
entries.description: Expansion pack for the original TrackMania with new tracks, cars, and game modes.

entries.title: TrackMania
entries.subtitle: 2003 · Windows
entries.description: Original TrackMania release focused on time-trial racing across the Desert and Rally environments.
